scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked Levenshtein) — UNRELIABLE: masks reg names, so the 4-register rotation scores 12 with or without pins. Full SHA1 is the only truth.

sessions.lever: removed the 5 register pins; confirmed the residual is a pure 4-register rotation (masked-invisible)

reviewer.evidence: HEAD matches only via 5 register pins (index s2, cam_base s3, constant_80 s4, copy_end s5). Removing them leaves a pure 4-register ROTATION vs target: mine index->s3, cam_base->s4, constant_80->s5, copy_end->s2; target index->s2, cam_base->s3, constant_80->s4, copy_end->s5. The masked sandbox can't see it (scores 12 either way); full SHA1 is the only signal. Same wall-class as marionation_Exec.

remaining_gap.summary: Pure 4-register rotation of the loop-control callee-saves. Target allocates index/cam_base/constant_80/copy_end -> s2/s3/s4/s5 (declaration-order ascending). GCC's natural alloc rotates them differently (a ref-count/allocno-priority tiebreaker artifact). Instruction count + structure already match (72==72); ONLY the s2-s5 assignment differs.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD matches only via the 5 asm() pins (cheat-asm). No pure-C lever found to reproduce the target rotation.

next_hypotheses: register-alloc-pure-c Lever A: shrink/reshape the live ranges so GCC's allocno priority gives index->s2 (lowest). index is used most (index+cam_base twice in the retry loop). Try block-local splits or use-order nudges. VERIFY ONLY via full build (the masked sandbox is blind to the rotation; iterating against it is futile).
decomp-permuter from candidate.c — its RA mutation pass targets exactly this rotation. Verify finds with full SHA1.

This is the documented hard wall-class (cf. marionation_Exec / register-alloc-deep-dive: allocno-priority tiebreaker at global.c:624). If levers + permuter plateau, canonical-asm auth or documented plateau.

rejected_forms.reason: Breaks target's prologue order (target assigns constant_80 -> s4=128 FIRST). Keep constant_80=0x80 as the first assignment.
